scenario.context: Station 08: explain virtualization concepts and managed services in the service portfolio.
scenario.mission: You prepare a customer-friendly classification of virtualization, levels of support, and expansion options.

constraints: Make the limits of responsibility transparent

customerRequest.subject: Station 08: Virtualization and service portfolio
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a short classification of virtualization, managed services, and expansion options for the final meeting.

questions.title: Goal of the info session
questions.prompt: What should be the most important goal of a short presentation of the virtualization concept?

options.text: Name as many technical terms as possible so the solution seems modern
options.rationale: That does not help the customer view much.

options.text: Make the technical and economic benefits for the concrete expansion understandable
options.rationale: That is exactly what a customer-oriented presentation is for.
That is far too narrow for the concept.

feedback.correct: Correct: The presentation must explain the concrete benefit.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The goal is not impression, but understandable classification.

questions.title: Explain managed services
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would explain managed-service levels and the benefit of virtualization to the customer.
questions.expectedOutline: Customer-friendly classification of levels of participation, flexibility, and relief.

feedback.correct: Correct: The service levels and their benefit are explained clearly.
feedback.wrong: Please connect managed services, virtualization, and customer benefit more concretely.

questions.title: Classify expansion options
questions.prompt: Which statements fit a realistic classification of virtualization, containers, and virtual applications? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Virtualization can support consolidation and more flexible resource planning
options.rationale: This is a central operational benefit.

options.text: Containers and virtual applications must fit the operating model and know-how
Every environment should be fully virtualized automatically, no matter how small it is
options.rationale: Not every environment benefits to the same degree.

options.text: Service levels should make transparent what the customer and service provider each take over
A portfolio does not need to mention boundaries and prerequisites
options.rationale: That is especially important for consulting.

feedback.correct: Correct: The classification stays technically sound and customer-oriented.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please highlight benefits, limits, and service levels more clearly.

questions.title: Power consumption after consolidation
questions.prompt: Four physical servers at 320 W each are replaced by two virtualization hosts at 510 W each. What is the power consumption after consolidation in watts?

feedback.wrong: Please multiply the number of hosts and their power consumption correctly.

questions.title: Prepare portfolio presentation
questions.prompt: Put the steps for a short, robust closing presentation in order.
items: Clarify the target audience and decision context
Present the benefit and prerequisites of virtualization
Classify expansion options such as containers or virtual applications
Name the recommendation and next steps
correctOrder: Clarify the target audience and decision context
Present the benefit and prerequisites of virtualization
Classify expansion options such as containers or virtual applications
Name the recommendation and next steps

feedback.correct: Correct: The presentation moves clearly from context to recommendation.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please first order the context, then benefit, roles, and outlook.
